title: ODI Matrix — Hotels (except Casino Hotels) and Motels

sections.title: Step 2 — Satisfaction Recalibration Notes
sections.content:
Incumbent mix: A1 Basic mechanical alarm 45% · A2 Wired panic bar 20% · A3 IoT monitoring 25% · A4 Guard patrol ~5% · A5 Do nothing 10%.

| Need | Original sat | Recalibrated | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| E10 — Alert latency <30 sec | 5 | **4** | Dominant A1 (45%) delivers zero remote notification; blended market satisfaction ≈ 3–4. IoT minority (25%) achieves it, but majority of installed base cannot. Lowered to 4. |
| All others | — | **unchanged** | Incumbent evidence confirms scores. E8 sat=8 (all certified products meet EN 179/1125). E15 sat=3 (no incumbent provides automated fire marshal log). E6/E9 sat=4 (A1 majority has no remote alert). |

---

sections.title: Step 4 — Market ODI Summary
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Market | Hotels (except Casino Hotels) and Motels |
| NAICS | 721110 |
| Total needs | 27 (19 market-level + 8 product-related) |
| **Underserved** (imp > 7 AND sat < 5) | **6** — E6, E7, E9, E10, E15, E18 |
| **Overserved** (imp < 3 AND sat > 7) | **0** |
| Avg opportunity score | **10.63** |
| ODI zone | Appropriately served (10–12) with high-OS underserved cluster |

### Top 5 Opportunities (by OS desc)

| Rank | ID | OS | Statement |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | E6 | **14** | Minimize the likelihood of an emergency exit remaining in an unauthorized open state for >5 min without triggering a staff alert |
| 2 | E9 | **14** | Increase the ability of a single security officer to monitor real-time exit door status across ≥50 exits from one centralized interface without patrol |
| 3 | E10 | **14** | Minimize the time from emergency exit activation to security staff notification to <30 seconds regardless of time of day |
| 4 | E5 | **13** | Increase the ability to verify every installed device transmits correctly and alarm operates without obstruction — within a single commissioning test |
| 5 | E15 | **13** | Increase the ability to produce a complete timestamped digital audit log formatted for fire marshal inspection without manual compilation |

---

sections.title: Step 5 — Cross-Check: ODI vs. Feature-Market Fit
sections.content:
Feature-Market Fit avg (from FIT_hotels_motels.md Step 09): `(5.56+5.33+6.22+5.56+6.44+4.89)/6 = **5.67**`

| Threshold | Condition | Status |
|---|---|---|
| HIGH opp (ODI avg > 12) but LOW fit (FMA < 4) | ODI avg = 10.63 → does NOT exceed 12 | No flag |
| LOW opp (ODI avg < 8) but HIGH fit (FMA > 7) | ODI avg = 10.63 → does NOT fall below 8 | No flag |

**No ODI–Feature-Fit inconsistency.** ODI avg 10.63 (appropriately served zone) and FMA 5.67 (moderate fit) are directionally consistent: the market has meaningful unmet needs but the product delivers moderate-to-strong coverage of the top underserved cluster.

**Key signal:** The 3-way tie at OS=14 (E6 / E9 / E10) represents the IoT dashboard value proposition directly — real-time detection, centralized monitoring, sub-30-sec alerts. These are all underserved by the dominant incumbent (A1, 45% basic mechanical) and constitute the primary go-to-market proof point for the IoT variant.

**Protocol lock-in watch:** C6/N6/N7 + Vendor Fit inferior verdict on BUS integration cluster — three aligned signals that EasyWave proprietary lock-in is the highest-friction product-related barrier. N6 OS=10 positions this in the appropriately-served zone but with low satisfaction (sat=4), meaning incremental improvement here drives meaningful opportunity uplift.

---

```json
        "feature_fit_avg": 5.67,
        "odi_avg": 10.63,
        "flag": "none",
        "note": "No inconsistency. ODI avg 10.63 (appropriately served) and FMA 5.67 (moderate) are directionally consistent."
      }
    }
  }
}
```

rows.Rationale: Dominant A1 (45%) delivers zero remote notification; blended market satisfaction ≈ 3–4. IoT minority (25%) achieves it, but majority of installed base cannot. Lowered to 4.
Incumbent evidence confirms scores. E8 sat=8 (all certified products meet EN 179/1125). E15 sat=3 (no incumbent provides automated fire marshal log). E6/E9 sat=4 (A1 majority has no remote alert).
